fact: June 15 sits exactly 166 days into the year, which means there are exactly 199 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date falls smack in the middle of peak firefly season - millions of tiny butts are lighting up backyards everywhere!
The sun rises before 5:30 AM in most northern cities on June 15, making it one of the earliest sunrise days of the entire year.
June 15 marks the time when many flowers release their strongest scents to attract bugs - your nose is basically experiencing plant perfume overload!
In Antarctica, June 15 brings some of the coldest, darkest days with temperatures that can freeze your spit before it hits the ground.
This date falls during Gemini season, when the constellation Gemini (the twins) is most visible in the night sky.
June 15 happens right when many trees are pumping out maximum oxygen - you're literally breathing the freshest air of the year!
In northern regions, the sun sets after 8 PM on this date, giving kids extra daylight hours to play outside after dinner.
Many animals are in full summer coat-shedding mode around June 15 - dogs, cats, and wild animals are basically walking fur factories!
The date sits just 6 days before the summer solstice, making it one of the longest days for shadow-chasing games.
In the Southern Hemisphere, June 15 brings the deep chill of winter with frost that can turn spider webs into glittery ice sculptures.
June 15 falls during peak thunderstorm season in many places - the atmosphere is basically throwing electric tantrums almost daily!
